item.title: Shipping Costs to Rise as Iran Conflict Continues, Maersk Boss Warns
item.description: The cost of shipping will rise due to the ongoing conflict in Iran, with the boss of Maersk warning that traditional contracting mechanisms will pass on fuel price fluctuations to customers.

item.title: Meta Urged to Boost Oversight of Fake AI Videos
item.description: Meta's Oversight Board has called on the company to boost oversight of fake AI videos on its platforms, citing concerns about the proliferation of such content during global military conflicts.

item.title: Russia Seeks Gains from Iran War Through Diplomacy and Economics
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is using the Iran conflict to boost his country's profile in the Gulf and Middle East, while also seeking economic benefits from the surge in global oil prices.

item.title: Fortnite Maker Raises V-Bucks Price Amid Increased Operating Costs
item.description: Epic Games raises the price of Fortnite's V-Bucks, citing increased operating costs, while removing bonus currency rewards from the game's main battle pass.

item.title: Signal Issues Warning to Users After Hackers Target Officials
item.description: Signal has warned users to be cautious of scams after Dutch intelligence agencies said hackers backed by Russia had targeted high-profile users of the secure messaging app. The company has urged users not to share their account details and to regularly check their devices for suspicious activity.

item.title: Electric Air Taxis Get Green Light for Widespread Testing
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has approved eight pilot programs for electric aircraft testing in 26 states, aiming to ensure U.S. companies lead the way in next-gen aircraft.
